### Post by Tony Flury on 2013-02-14
Can you be more specific about what you mean when you say - "Clip the sprite sheet".

I think one of the reasons you are not getting many answers is that people aren't clear exactly what you are asking.

Are you asking for a method where you can get a sprite to animate a cell at a time from a multi-celled image ?

### Post by snowlizard31 on 2013-02-14
How do I separate each of the stick man into a list so that I can create an animation when the player moves? Do I even use a list?

---

### Post by Tony Flury on 2013-02-15
> **snowlizard31 said:**
> How do I separate each of the stick man into a list so that I can create an animation when the player moves? Do I even use a list?

Ok - you need to break this down into a number of parts (this is how my prototype animated sprite code works :


[LIST=1]
[*]Make sure your "sheet" is regular - i.e. each sprite is the same size and with the spacing between them - I started by creating a regular grid, and then making sure my image was inside the cell.
[*]Write a function which when given the dimensions of the grid (i.e. size of the cell, gaps between them number of cells horizontally and vertically) can work out the topx and topy (and bottomx and bottomy) of each cell
[*]Load the entire image file
[*]build a list consisting of a number of pygame surfaces each one the same size as a single cell of your animation. Each Surface in the list is actually a subsurface of the main image, with the position calculated in function 
[*]Now as you update/move your sprite on the screen you apply the appropriate surface from your pre-built list to the sprite so as to animate the sprite.
[/LIST]

My code is a bit more involved than above as I have allowed my Sprites to have a "state" and i can identify which cells apply to which state and how rapidly they are "played" and in what order, and I have built it as a sub class of sprite to make it easy to use, and my code is still very prototype and buggy (it ocassionally fails on state changes etc - but the general principle works.

---

### Post by snowlizard31 on 2013-02-17
Step 2 is the step I'm having a problem with how to i determine how the gaps between each sprite in the sheet and the location on the sheet?

---

### Post by Tony Flury on 2013-02-17
> **snowlizard31 said:**
> Step 2 is the step I'm having a problem with how to i determine how the gaps between each sprite in the sheet and the location on the sheet?

You determine that when you draw the pictures, which is why I suggest that you draw a grid first and make sure you individual cells fit into the cells in the grid.. I pass these as parameters to my function when I build the list of surfaces. There is no simple way you can automatically detect the right gaps etc just from the image..

---

### Post by Tony Flury on 2013-02-18
> **Tony Flury said:**
> You determine that when you draw the pictures, which is why I suggest that you draw a grid first and make sure you individual cells fit into the cells in the grid.. I pass these as parameters to my function when I build the list of surfaces. There is no simple way you can automatically detect the right gaps etc just from the image..

If you can wait a few days - maybe a bit longer - I will complete a project that is currently very rough which will automatically generate a picture grid for animation cells. I have an idea also to add tags to the picture so that a suitable written code can automatically read the grid dimensions from the picture - Watch this space :-).
